title: Cross-board dependencies
excerpt:
Hello, 
Could we enable cross-board dependencies? I am working on a relatively complex set of tasks and using different boards to avoid over-cluttered boards, but this prevents me from flowing dependencies back to a ‘sum…

posts.author: n.renard (Nic Renard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

Could we enable cross-board dependencies? I am working on a relatively complex set of tasks and using different boards to avoid over-cluttered boards, but this prevents me from flowing dependencies back to a ‘summary board’.

Could this be a feature that gets added? Alternatively, is there a workaround that is simple (i.e. not requiring a ton of mirrored/linked columns)?

Thank you,
Best,
Nic

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @n.renard, Nic

I am currently working on an app with a feature that might help here. Let me know your exact needs and there is a chance I can incorporate this in the app. Currently my app does:

image1236×134 12.5 KB

So, there is a relation between a main and a sub board. The sub board need to have something like [xyz] in its title and in the main board there should be a column where an item holds “xyz”. With this I can build a relation between an item in the main board and a group in a sub board.

The first one will report the % of status in the group (sub board) to the item (main board) but you can also think of average number.

posts.author: mitchell.hudson (Mitchell hudson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @n.renard,

It sounds like you could achieve this by using a dashboard.

What information are you trying to display on your ‘summary’ board?

posts.author: n.renard (Nic Renard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @basdebruin,

Thanks for reaching out. What we’re looking for is the ability to create dependencies across different boards. We are developing a prototype of a new technology and I have one board where I capture all my manufacturing steps for various units, and another where I capture the test schedule for these different units.

I would like to be able to show that until I have completed all the manufacturing steps in the manufacturing board on unit X, I cannot start testing unit X - and then also highlight that testing can start once all the manufacturing steps are shown as complete.

Similarly, I would like to have a summary board where I can track at top level progress of each unit through manufacturing, testing, etc. That I might indeed be able to do it with dashboard, I need to explore that a little more.

Would your app enable this?

Thank you!
Best,
Nic

posts.author: n.renard (Nic Renard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @mitchell.hudson,

Thanks for your reply. A dashboard might indeed be able to do that, I need to play with those a little more.

What I’m not certain dashboards can do is provide enough flexibility to combine different groups or line items together. We are developing a prototype of a new technology and I have one board where I capture all my manufacturing steps for various units, and another where I capture the test schedule for these different units. I would like to have a summary board where I can show how well unit A is progressing through steps X, Y and Z of manufacturing, which could be three line items in the summary board under a group called ‘Unit A’ (with a rule showing if specific line item in manufacturing board is shown as complete, then show step X as complete in summary board, etc.) and steps Q, R and S of testing (using similar rule as before). Does that makes sense? I don’t believe this can be achieved through dashboards?

Thank you,
Best,
Nic

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @n.renard

My app (in development) is currently not working with dependencies and I really doubt if something like multi board dependencies can be achieved. To be able to comment on that I need some more details, maybe a screenshot of your boards can help.

For your second request (the summary) you should eb able to use a dashboard. The app I was referring to is build to overcome a limitation in dashboards (max nr of boards = 10) and my app reports a status from a group to an item in a summary board. As an example: in your detail board you have a group with 20 items and 15 items are set to done. My app will simply show “75% Done” in a column / item in the summary board. I am working on other columns (eg report an average number from a group) but no ideas for dependencies yet.

You can also consider

to link items from a detailed board back to a summary board.

posts.author: n.renard (Nic Renard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @basdebruin,

I will check out the dashboards and this automation in more detail. Thanks for the feedback!

Best,
Nic

posts.author: curiousjo (J Berg)
posts.cookedHtml: I would like this too. Has there been any progress?

posts.author: rkrestin (Ruth Krestin)
posts.cookedHtml: Are cross board dependencies on the roadmap at all? they’re super critical for our company

posts.author: apastor (Ashish Pastor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Team,

I already see this submitted as an idea way back in May’21 ( Dependencies across multiple boards) but was closed saying that it was on radar. This is an important feature that will help people big time. There can be multiple projects under same workspace / stream for which multiple boards might need to be created, and making the tasks from one board dependent on another task on another board is a big need in this situation. Kindly suggest the plan to have this small feature.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you for your feature request. We hear you and recognize there’s demand for this feature. As such, we have merged your feature request with another similar request here so that the votes can be combined! I encourage you to vote on the topic as well to put emphasis on this need as every quarter we take the features with the top votes to our development team for a response. Feel free to have a look around the category and see other features you would like to vote on, and thank you for your understanding in advance Your opinion matters to us!

posts.author: tmanee (Tyler Manee)
posts.cookedHtml:
Are cross-board item-level dependencies in the works? Frequently in project management a multi-department organization might have a task for one project not able to begin until another department finishes a certain task.

I know there are workarounds and ways to ‘get it done’ without cross board dependency, but they’re not smooth or elegant. I would like to be able to select any item in my workspace for a dependency, not just items in the same board.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @tmanee,

Thanks for your feature request! At this time, we don’t have update to share at this stage in regard to cross-board dependencies. That being said, we encourage you to cast your vote on the existing feature request here as we have merged your request into this request to combine votes and put emphasis on this need Every quarter we take the features with the top votes to our development team for a response. Your opinion matters to us, so please get familiar with this section and add your vote to feature requests that interest you most!

posts.author: kaylak (Kayla K)
posts.cookedHtml:
@BiancaT Should these two topics be merged to ensure accurate number of votes on this topic?
https://community.monday.com/t/dependencies-between-different-boards/42945/2

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you for raising this @kaylak

I have merged the request into this one to ensure we have votes combined to put emphasis on this need! Thank you again!

posts.author: slissajoux (LISSAJOUX)
posts.cookedHtml: Would it be possible to select an item from another board in a “Dependant on” column ?

posts.author: mark.anley (Green Llamas Consulting)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @slissajoux - no this is not possible in standard monday. However, as a workaround, if the items are connected (via connect boards column) and the date from the other board is mirrored. You could use an app like Column Magic or General Caster to copy the mirrored date value to a local date column on the board and setup your dependency against that column.

Thanks,
Mark

Green Llamas

monday.com + Green Llamas

Green Llamas is here to help you make all your People and Projects Green. Let us get your herds under control using the monday.com platform.

posts.author: alonbl (Alon Bar-Lev)
posts.cookedHtml: Dependencies is essential feature of project management, not having the option to define dependencies between any object to any object with different kind of dependencies (or relationship) shows that this tool is not mature for project management. Mirroring is not a valid solution.

posts.author: ViktorJacobs (Viktor)
posts.cookedHtml: I just want to chime in to the already loud choir; cross-board dependencies is a crucial function for our continued use of Monday. We are management consultants who often refer to and implement Monday for our customers when it’s time to implement the roadmap we’ve identified for their needs. These are full of projects and are often not suitable to have in one single board. They do however have a lot of interconnecting dependencies, and not being able to connect them across different boards is a hassle. Can we get it, pretty please?

posts.author: Work_Perfect_Apps (Work Perfect Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Viktor,
We are working on an app that will solve for cross board dependencies. Would you be interested in having a chat with our Apps lead to discuss further on this topic.

Please drop an email on appsupport@workperfect.io to have a chat.

Thanks !

posts.author: IvanB (Ivan Barankevich)
posts.cookedHtml: This is something that would be very important to our group. We have several engineering groups that are using monday.com. Electrical, Mechanical, Controls. Lets say the mechanical engineers start the project first, when they get to a certain point that’s when the electrical engineers can start their work, when they get to a certain point the Controls guys can start. We have very large projects so we have to have separate boards for the different Engineering groups, so either having cross board dependencies, or being able to bi-directionally mirror certain tasks. Column mirroring doesn’t help us since we only need to mirror one or two tasks and with columns you are adding a bunch of columns that are pretty much useless for most of the project.

posts.author: LipinsM (Mike)
posts.cookedHtml: Also looking to use this critical feature as I similarly have department based boards that have dependent tasks in other departments. Would like to display these cross board dependencies on a Gantt chart dashboard view connecting 10 different boards.

posts.author: jrmfzf (Jarrod)
posts.cookedHtml: This really is a critical feature for managing large portfolios. I manage a utility where a shared pool of resources are used across multiple different programs. I can manage the different programs by creating unique boards for each. However because of the shared resources a delay in a project from one program will almost always impact other projects outside of that program. The ability shift the schedules of all the projects automatically is absolutely necessary for me to use Monday effectively.

posts.author: kranthi_thoughtflow (Kranthi Kiran)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @n.renard - this might be a great use case for our app Birds eye view for monday.com .
Although it is not a substitute for the cross board connections between boards, it allows you to visualize these relationships ( across multiple levels of boards ) on a single screen- making it much easier to manage the complexity.

Let me know if this does the job. Would really appreciate your feedback.

posts.author: astedtler (Anna Stedtler)
posts.cookedHtml: PLEASE PLEASE make this a feature!!! The versatility that this would allow would be INCREDIBLE!!!

posts.author: NCE (Natalie Costas-Evangelou)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there any further update on this requested feature? We have a number of strategic programmes where this could be heavily utilised.

posts.author: Ozzy_O (Ozzy Osborne)
posts.cookedHtml: 100% agree - the amount of workarounds, specialized automations and/or 3rd party apps to achieve basic project management functionality consistently is amazing.

posts.author: tnaumann (Tate Naumann)
posts.cookedHtml: I truly cannot believe that this is still not a feature, all these requests combined to a total of 64 votes, 64 votes most likely from different organizations that need this feature. We already pay for this software to help manage our projects and it baffles me that in at least 4 years Monday.com has not added this feature, a feature that should be basic. I have many stakeholders within my company that need me to find a solution for this without paying for a 3rd party application. I doubt that I am the only one among these 64 votes that has faced this issue. I strongly urge someone in the @CSG to push this for one of the releases for this next quarter it is important in order for my company to remain a Monday.com customer for the next 5 years.

posts.author: rbailey (Rachel Bailey)
posts.cookedHtml: How is this not on the product Roadmap yet? What do I need to do to get it on the Roadmap? MUST HAVE!!!
